data.title: Catalight Academy Grows Leading Nonprofit's Autism and I/DD Clinical Training Capability
data.description: In March 6, 2026, in Walnut Creek, California, an expanded remote learning platform named Catalight Academy was introduced, providing modern, evidence-based care on a global scale. This initiative aims to broaden access to education by offering a diverse range of digital courses. The platform offers an increased number of resources and tools to support learners, including live sessions and interactive content. With this expansion, Catalight Academy plans to reach a larger audience and make high-quality education more accessible worldwide.

data.title: Bermuda Risk Summit 2026 to Spotlight Conflagration Risk as Wildfire Losses Reshape Global Insurance Markets
data.description: Hamilton, Bermuda - On March 6, 2026, the Bermuda Business Development Agency (BDA) revealed plans for two highlighted sessions at the Bermuda Risk Summit 2026. These sessions will concentrate on wildfire risk mitigation strategies and will feature a keynote address along with an expert panel discussion. The event aims to delve into integrated underwriting, mitigation, and capital management strategies, with a particular focus on addressing the challenges posed by escalating wildfire incidents. Participants will explore innovative approaches to managing these risks and enhancing resilience in the face of increasing threats.

data.title: A16z Crypto To Launch Fifth Fund Targeting $2 Billion
data.description: A venture firm, A16z crypto, the blockchain-focused arm of Andreessen Horowitz, is currently in the process of raising funds for its fifth fund. Sources have revealed that the firm is targeting approximately $2 billion for this fund, which will center its investments solely on blockchain technology. In a related development, the shares of Grayscale Ethereum Staking ETF are facing a decline, prompting questions about the reason behind the fall in ETHE stock. The fundraising efforts are anticipated to be finalized by the conclusion of the first half of 2026. Over the past few months, A16z has been actively engaging in investments within the crypto space, with recent involvement in companies like Babylon, a Bitcoin lending protocol. Additionally, the firm declared its intention to raise $2.5 billion in funding, as reported on Benzinga.com.

data.title: First Western Trust Appoints Jeff Miller as Scottsdale Market President
data.description: First Western Trust, a subsidiary of First Western Financial, Inc. (NASDAQ: MYFW), has named Jeff Miller as the new Scottsdale Market President. Miller, who has over 15 years of experience in commercial and private banking, is known for his extensive lending knowledge, exceptional leadership skills, and close connections to the Scottsdale community.

data.title: Egan-Jones Releases March 2026 CLO Market Summary
data.description: On March 6, 2026, Egan-Jones released its March 2026 CLO Summary Report analyzing the collateralized loan obligation (CLO) market. The report highlighted that CLO issuance stayed low despite market conditions. It delved into issuance trends, credit quality indicators, and structural metrics as of March. The report did not provide specific numbers on issuance but discussed the overall subdued trend in the market. Egan-Jones noted various factors impacting the CLO market in their comprehensive review.

data.title: KBRA Assigns AAA Rating, Stable Outlook to DASNY State Personal Income Tax Revenue Bonds (General Purpose) Series 2026A (Tax-Exempt) and Series 2026B (Federally Taxable)
data.description: NEW YORK-- In a recent assessment, KBRA has given a long-term rating of AAA with a Stable Outlook to the Dormitory Authority of the State of New York (DASNY) State Personal Income Tax Revenue Bonds. This rating applies to Series 2026A (Tax-Exempt) and Series 2026B (Federally Taxable). Additionally, KBRA has maintained the AAA rating and Stable Outlook on existing State Personal Income Tax Bonds issued by DASNY and the New York State Thruway Authority. This rating action takes into consideration significant credit factors.

data.title: CFO sentenced for diverting $35M from company to personal DeFi platform, resulting in loss during Terra collapse.
data.description: An individual, Nevin Shetty, discreetly transferred $35 million from company funds to his personal DeFi platform. Unfortunately, the entirety of this amount was subsequently lost in the Terra collapse.
